pi_items.verified_value: 2.0 degrees - Non-standard but intentional custom tolerance for task flexibility
pi_items.source: ISO 2768 standard defines 'very coarse' angular tolerance as ±1° to ±3° depending on dimension size; 2.0° is a custom loose tolerance chosen for this task

pi_items.verified_value: 10.0 mm - Non-standard but intentional custom tolerance for task flexibility
pi_items.source: ISO 2768 defines 'very coarse' linear tolerance up to ±4mm for dimensions over 400mm; 10.0mm is a custom loose tolerance chosen for this task to allow verification flexibility

privileged_info_summary: The isometric fire riser task uses verified technical drawing standards: angles 90°, 150°, 30°, and 270° correctly represent vertical and isometric projections with the 30°/150° pair being standard isometric axes. All DXF layer color codes (1=Red, 4=Cyan, 7=White) are correct AutoCAD Color Index values. The geometry specifications (1000mm vertical, 1200mm isometric, 800mm isometric, 300mm drop) create a realistic fire riser pipe configuration. Custom tolerances of ±2° and ±10mm are loose by ISO 2768 standards but intentionally chosen to provide verification flexibility. The task creates synthetic output via LibreCAD user actions, verified by the ezdxf Python library for DXF parsing.
